Patch Set 3:

To test this, need to actually build the readiness docker, upload it to dockerhub and change the references everywhere to new docker image...
Would you like to do that?

Patch Set 3:

> To test this, need to actually build the readiness docker, upload
 > it to dockerhub and change the references everywhere to new docker
 > image...
 > Would you like to do that?

Instead of changing the repo/image everywhere I used following to test it:
1. Create the image locally (I can provide the Dockerfile) or pull it from Dockerhub (my repo for now) on all nodes of K8s cluster. I can upload the image on Dockerhub then you can pull it manually.
2. Retag it as actual image oomk8s/readiness-check:2.0.0
3. Install using --set global.pullPolicy=IfNotPresent

What do you think?

Patch Set 3:

@Mahendra, we will need to build the image and push to docker hub before we can merge. I will reach out to you to work through this.
Thanks,
Mike.

Patch Set 4:

M3

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Not sparking much joy

Patch Set 4:

> (1 comment)
 > 
 > Not sparking much joy

Keong Lim, James MacNider, James Forsyth, Venkata Harish Kajur : This change will provide the flexibility to use ready.py with container name of Jobs/statefulsets/deployments and check the readiness (in case of job completion) even during the upgrades. At the same time, we have job_complete.py intact and if applications want to use that explicitly, there is no stopping. It will be the end developer who would decide which script to use.

I believe this change can go in. Changes in the AAI rolling upgrade related to readiness of jobs will be reverted to job_complete.py.

Thanks, 
Amit
